message:
fix(sync): preserve tombstones across --rebuild's delegated auto-recovery path too

Commit 97a085c fixed tombstone preservation in the in-place
`execute_import` rebuild path (which already had the
`snapshot_tombstones` → `reset_data_tables` → `restore_tombstones`
sequence). But sync also delegates to `recover_database_from_jsonl`
when `--rebuild` fires against an existing-DB-plus-existing-JSONL
state — and THAT path opens a fresh DB and imports only what's in
the JSONL, so tombstones in the old DB but not yet flushed to
JSONL are silently lost.

Fix: snapshot tombstones from `open_result.storage` BEFORE calling
`recover_database_from_jsonl`, then restore them onto the
post-delegation storage. Matches the sequence the non-delegated
path already runs.

  preserved = snapshot_tombstones(storage)?;
  open_result.recover_database_from_jsonl()?;
  if !preserved.is_empty() {
      restore_tombstones(&open_result.storage, &preserved)?;
      debug!(count = preserved.len(),
             "Restored tombstones across delegated auto-recovery rebuild");
  }

The debug log mirrors the in-place path's log line so `br
--log-level=debug sync --rebuild` produces the same trace
regardless of which rebuild path ran.

Cargo.lock intentionally not committed — local path-override
artifact.
